name: Farmers Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Community bank operating across Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas with FDIC insurance, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and business banking services.
description_long:
Farmers Bank & Trust is a regional community bank serving Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas with a focus on personal relationships and local community involvement. The bank emphasizes its core values of Honor, Excellence, Adaptability, Respect, and Teamwork (HEART) in all customer and community interactions. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection for customers.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, mortgages, online banking, bill pay, and business solutions through Autobooks. They provide 24/7 account access through their website and mobile platforms, as well as ATM services through their MyFarmers iTeller (ITM) locations, which combine automated and live teller services. Additional offerings include merchant services, treasury management, and trust services through Community First Trust, described as Arkansas' first and only independent trust company.

Farmers Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through strong community focus and personal service. The bank highlights customer testimonials emphasizing personalized attention (being called by name), family-friendly atmosphere, and special accommodations like designated parking for expectant mothers and veterans. Their MyStart program targets younger customers with checking, savings, and credit-building products. The institution maintains an active blog and podcast (Right at the HEART Blog) for customer education and community updates.

As a regional community bank, Farmers Bank & Trust is best suited for customers in its service areas seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making. The main limitation is geographic restriction to Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas—customers outside these states cannot access their services. The bank's product offerings are standard for community banks, without premium features or rates that might attract rate-sensitive or specialty finance customers.
services: MyStart youth checking and savings with credit-building features
Online banking with 24/7 account access and bill pay
Business banking and accounting through Autobooks
Merchant services and digital payment acceptance
Trust company services through Community First Trust
24/7 ATM access through MyFarmers iTeller locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
24/7 access to accounts, bill pay, and online banking services
MyFarmers iTeller provides both ATM and live teller assistance at select locations
Personalized service model emphasizing customer relationships and community involvement
MyStart youth banking program with checking, savings, and credit-building features
Community First Trust offers independent trust company services in Arkansas
Autobooks business banking tool includes invoicing and digital payment acceptance
Multiple service channels including online, mobile, and physical branches
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas—inaccessible to customers outside these states
No information provided on competitive interest rates for savings or CDs compared to national banks
Limited transparency on mortgage rates, loan terms, and fee schedules on homepage
No mention of specialized products like investment services, wealth management, or alternative lending options
Website provides limited detail on minimum account balances or service charges
best_for: Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas residents seeking relationship-based community banking with personalized service
Young adults building credit for the first time through the MyStart program
Small business owners needing invoicing, payment processing, and local business banking support
Homebuyers in the bank's service areas looking for mortgage services with local decision-making
diagnosis: Farmers Bank & Trust is best for residents of Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas seeking relationship-based community banking with personalized service and local decision-making. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—this bank is completely inaccessible to customers outside its three-state service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
